verbGlossary.ACTIVATES: {source} triggers a protective response when {target} event occurs (e.g., fire alarm activates mitigation action by initiating evacuation sequence)
verbGlossary.AGGRAVATES: {source} activity worsens an existing environmental or site condition (e.g., dewatering aggravates environmental {target} by discharging contaminated groundwater)
verbGlossary.ARC-FLASHES UNDER: {source} creates arc-flash hazard during energized work, posing safety {target} (e.g., power distribution arc-flashes under safety risk during panel terminations)
verbGlossary.AWAITS DELIVERY FOR: {source} installation is blocked pending material arrival, exposing the project to supply chain {target} (e.g., water main awaits delivery for long-lead pipe)
verbGlossary.BACKORDERED UNDER: {source} equipment is unavailable from manufacturers, creating supply chain {target} (e.g., air handling units backordered under supply chain risk from global shortages)
verbGlossary.BEARS LOAD PER: {source} is sized and designed to carry structural loads, inheriting design {target} if calculations are wrong (e.g., shallow foundation bears load per design risk assumptions)
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED UNDER: {source} must receive third-party certification or authority approval before occupancy (e.g., sprinkler mains certified under regulatory and permit {target} for fire marshal sign-off)
verbGlossary.CLASHES WITH: {source} physically conflicts with another system in the 3D model or field, creating coordination {target} (e.g., beams clash with MEP runs in ceiling plenum)
verbGlossary.CODE-REVIEWED UNDER: {source} must satisfy plumbing, mechanical, or building code before inspection sign-off (e.g., waste and vent code-reviewed under regulatory and permit {target})
verbGlossary.COLLIDES WITH: {source} occupies the same spatial zone as another system, forcing field rework (e.g., ductwork collides with structural members, creating coordination {target})
verbGlossary.CONCEALS: {source} covers prior work, making latent defects undetectable after placement (e.g., backfill conceals geotechnical {target} by burying soil conditions)
verbGlossary.CONCENTRATES: {source} focuses a disproportionate share of project cost or {target} into a single scope area (e.g., deep foundations concentrate risk exposure in early phases)
verbGlossary.CONFLICTS WITH: {source} routing competes for the same pathway or space as other systems (e.g., conduit conflicts with ductwork in coordination {target} zones)
verbGlossary.CRACKS UNDER: {source} develops physical failure when subjected to {target} conditions such as settlement or thermal movement (e.g., slab on grade cracks under differential settlement triggers)
verbGlossary.DELAYED BY: {source} cannot be installed on time because a supply chain event has pushed delivery out (e.g., casework delayed by supply chain {target} from custom millwork lead times)
verbGlossary.DELAYS DUE TO: {source} installation is held up because {target} condition materializes (e.g., deep foundations delayed due to schedule risk from unexpected refusal)
verbGlossary.DEPENDS ON FABRICATION FOR: {source} requires off-site manufacturing with long lead times, creating supply chain dependency (e.g., curtain wall depends on fabrication for custom extrusions)
verbGlossary.DETERIORATES INTO: {source} degrades over time if improperly installed, converting a latent defect into {target} (e.g., air barrier deteriorates into residual risk if laps are unsealed)
verbGlossary.DIVERTS RUNOFF PER: {source} channels water away from the site to meet environmental compliance (e.g., storm drainage diverts runoff per environmental {target} controls)
verbGlossary.DOWNGRADES: {source} installation reduces the assessed severity of {target} (e.g., fire pump downgrades risk score for fire suppression adequacy)
verbGlossary.ENCOUNTERS: {source} directly contacts or disturbs a subsurface condition, surfacing a geotechnical {target} (e.g., excavation encounters unknown soil or rock)
verbGlossary.ERECTED UNDER: {source} is assembled at height or under load, with installation governed by execution {target} controls (e.g., columns erected under construction execution risk protocols)
verbGlossary.ERODES: {source} delay or rework chips away at available float in the project schedule (e.g., late window deliveries erode schedule float)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES CREWS TO: {source} installation puts workers in proximity to fall, struck-by, or other jobsite hazards (e.g., roof structure exposes crews to fall safety {target})
verbGlossary.EXTENDS INTO: {source} activity overruns its planned duration and spills into schedule reserve time (e.g., TAB extends into schedule buffer when systems do not balance)
verbGlossary.FAILS UNDER: {source} does not perform as intended when subjected to construction-phase stresses or weather (e.g., flashing fails under construction execution from improper lapping)
verbGlossary.FRACTURES UNDER: {source} breaks or chips during or after installation due to improper substrate preparation or loading (e.g., floor tile fractures under construction execution from uneven substrate)
verbGlossary.GATES: {source} controls the start of downstream work, making it a schedule bottleneck (e.g., floor slabs gate interior rough-in trades)
verbGlossary.HOLDS CRITICAL PATH IN: {source} sits on the critical path and its delay directly extends the project completion date (e.g., elevator car holds critical path in schedule {target})
verbGlossary.INTEGRATES ACROSS: {source} must coordinate with multiple other systems and trades, concentrating coordination {target} at the integration point (e.g., building automation integrates across HVAC, lighting, and fire alarm, creating coordination risk at system boundaries)
verbGlossary.INSPECTED UNDER: {source} must pass regulatory inspection before it can be covered or put into service (e.g., sanitary sewer inspected under regulatory permit requirements)
verbGlossary.INTRODUCES: {source} brings a new category of hazard onto the project (e.g., gas service introduces safety {target} from flammable material on site)
verbGlossary.LONG-LEAD UNDER: {source} requires extended procurement lead time, making it vulnerable to supply chain disruption (e.g., elevator machinery is long-lead under supply chain {target})
verbGlossary.MANDATED BY: {source} is required by code or authority having jurisdiction, not optional (e.g., emergency power mandated by regulatory and permit {target} for life safety)
verbGlossary.MISALIGNS UNDER: {source} fails to fit as designed when field conditions deviate from drawings (e.g., connections misalign under design {target} from tolerance stack-up)
verbGlossary.MISCONFIGURED UNDER: {source} is incorrectly specified or programmed due to incomplete design information (e.g., HVAC controls misconfigured under design {target} from late sequence of operations)
verbGlossary.POSES: {source} introduces a hazard to workers during installation or construction (e.g., excavation poses safety {target} from trench collapse)
verbGlossary.PRESSURE-TESTED UNDER: {source} must pass a field test before concealment, with failure triggering execution {target} (e.g., domestic water pressure-tested under construction execution risk protocol)
verbGlossary.PUNCTURED BY: {source} is physically damaged by other trades working on or through it, triggering {target} event (e.g., roof membrane punctured by mechanical penetrations)
verbGlossary.REQUIRES CLEARANCE FROM: {source} cannot proceed until a regulatory or permit authority grants approval (e.g., dewatering requires clearance from permit authority for discharge)
verbGlossary.REQUIRES SKILLED CREW FOR: {source} installation demands specialized trade labor that may be in short supply (e.g., precast panels require skilled crew for rigging and setting)
verbGlossary.REVEALS: {source} work uncovers previously hidden defects in underlying systems (e.g., paint reveals residual {target} from drywall finishing defects)
verbGlossary.RUPTURES UNDER: {source} fails catastrophically, creating an immediate safety hazard on the jobsite (e.g., gas piping ruptures under safety {target} from excavation damage)
verbGlossary.SAFEGUARDS AGAINST: {source} is installed specifically to prevent or reduce an environmental or site {target} (e.g., erosion control safeguards against stormwater environmental risk)
verbGlossary.SEALS AGAINST: {source} provides a protective barrier that prevents moisture or environmental intrusion, reducing residual {target} (e.g., below-grade waterproofing seals against residual water intrusion risk)
verbGlossary.SECURES AGAINST: {source} provides physical protection that reduces a safety hazard on site (e.g., fencing secures against safety {target} from unauthorized site access)
verbGlossary.SEQUENCED BY: {source} installation order is dictated by schedule dependencies that carry {target} if disrupted (e.g., drywall sequenced by schedule risk after MEP rough-in)
verbGlossary.SPLIT ACROSS: {source} scope is divided among multiple responsible parties, creating {target} allocation complexity (e.g., site electrical split across risk allocation between GC and utility company)
verbGlossary.STIPULATED BY: {source} installation standards are specified as a condition of project insurance coverage (e.g., grounding stipulated by insurance requirement for lightning protection)
verbGlossary.TOLERANCES CHALLENGED BY: {source} has tight dimensional requirements that are jeopardized when design details are incomplete or conflicting (e.g., curtain wall tolerances challenged by design {target} in embed locations)
